subject: LIGO/Virgo s190728q: ePESSTO+ NTT classification of AT2019lvt as a type-Ia SN
submitter: Christopher Frohmaier at Inst. of Cosmology and Gravitation  <chris.frohmaier@port.ac.uk>
body:
P. Wiseman, C. Angus (Southampton), S. Benetti (INAF), M. Gromadzki
(Warsaw), S. Srivastav, S. Smartt, J. Gillanders, O. McBrien, K.
Smith, D. Young (QUB), R. Carini, M. T. Botticella, E. Brocato, E.
Cappellaro, P. D Avanzo, J. Anderson (ESO), T. Muller Bravo
(Southampton), T.-W. Chen (MPE), C. Inserra (Cardiff), E. Kankare
(Turku), M. Nicholl (Edinburgh), O. Yaron (Weizmann), C. Frohmaier, E.
Swann (ICG; Portsmouth), S. Taubenberger (MPA-Garching), P. Pessi
(IALP), I. Mandel (Monash/Birmingham), L. Galbany (U. Pittsburgh), Z.
Kostrzewa-Rutkowska (SRON), Ken Chambers, Mark Huber (IfA)

ePESSTO+, the advanced Public ESO Spectroscopic Survey for Transient
Objects (see Smartt et al. 2015, A&A, 579, 40 http://www.pessto.org ),
reports the following spectroscopic classifications of transients in
the skymap of GW event s190728q (GCN 25187). Targets were supplied by
DECam-GROWTH (GCN 25215).

All observations were performed on the ESO New Technology Telescope at
La Silla on 2019 07 29, using EFOSC2 and Grism 13 (3985-9315A, 18A
resolution).

Classifications were done with SNID (Blondin & Tonry, 2007, ApJ, 666,
1024). Classification spectra and additional details can be obtained
from http://www.pessto.org (via WISeREP) and the IAU Transient Name
Server.

Survey Name | IAU Name | RA (J2000) | Dec (J2000) | Disc. Date |
Source    | Disc Mag | z   | Type | Phase |
DG19blknb  | AT2019lvt | 21 13 58.27 | 14 47 13.08 | 20190729 |
DECam-GROWTH | 19.63  | 0.255 | Ia | -6d |
